questions.q: Whose wife was Ahalya?
questions.explanation: Ahalya was the wife of the great sage Gautama.

questions.q: What punishment did Ahalya suffer as a curse?
options: She was turned into a river
questions.explanation: Ahalya was cursed to lie as stone for many years until her release.

questions.q: Who finally released Ahalya from her curse?
questions.explanation: Rama's arrival and the touch of the dust of his feet released Ahalya.

questions.q: Which god deceived Ahalya, leading to her curse?
questions.explanation: Indra, king of the gods, deceived Ahalya, which brought about Gautama's curse.

questions.q: By what means was Ahalya restored to her human form?
questions.explanation: The dust of Rama's feet touching the stone restored Ahalya to her form.

questions.q: What is Ahalya most famously remembered as in the Ramayana?
options: A cursed woman set free by Rama
questions.explanation: Ahalya is remembered as the cursed woman whom Rama liberated.

questions.q: In whose company did Rama come to Ahalya's hermitage?
questions.explanation: Rama and Lakshmana were traveling with the sage Vishvamitra when they reached the hermitage.

questions.q: Who else accompanied Rama when Ahalya was freed?
questions.explanation: Rama's brother Lakshmana accompanied him on the journey with Vishvamitra.

questions.q: Ahalya's curse was lifted during which phase of Rama's life?
questions.explanation: Ahalya was freed in Rama's youth, before his marriage, while escorting Vishvamitra.

questions.q: What kind of dwelling did Ahalya and Gautama have?
questions.explanation: Ahalya lived with the sage Gautama at his forest hermitage.

questions.q: How did Indra disguise himself to deceive Ahalya, according to tradition?
options: In the form of the sage Gautama
questions.explanation: Indra took on the appearance of Gautama himself to deceive Ahalya.

questions.q: Who pronounced the curse upon Ahalya?
questions.explanation: Her husband, the sage Gautama, pronounced the curse upon discovering the deceit.

questions.q: Toward which city was Vishvamitra leading the princes when they passed Ahalya's hermitage?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra led Rama toward Mithila for King Janaka's bow ceremony, passing the hermitage on the way.

questions.q: According to tradition, who is the divine creator said to have fashioned Ahalya?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Brahma created Ahalya as a being of exceptional beauty.

questions.q: What did Indra suffer as part of Gautama's curse upon him?
questions.explanation: Gautama cursed Indra as well, traditionally so that he bore disfiguring marks or lost his virility.

questions.q: What did Ahalya do during her long years under the curse?
options: Sang hymns aloud at the gate
Endured penance, lying invisible and unseen
questions.explanation: She remained at the hermitage, invisible to all and enduring austerity until Rama's arrival.

questions.q: After Ahalya was freed, what did Gautama do?
options: He left for the Himalayas forever
questions.explanation: Gautama returned to his hermitage and was reunited with the purified Ahalya.

questions.q: Ahalya is counted among the revered women known as the Panchakanya. What does that group signify?
options: Five maidens whose names purify those who recite them
questions.explanation: Ahalya is one of the Panchakanya, five honored women whose remembrance is held to absolve sin.

questions.q: Why was Vishvamitra escorting Rama and Lakshmana on this journey?
options: On the way to Janaka's court for the bow ceremony
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra was escorting the princes toward Mithila, where Rama would later win Sita.

questions.q: What did the gods and sages do after Rama restored Ahalya?
questions.explanation: Celestial beings and sages praised Rama and celebrated Ahalya's restoration.

questions.q: What does the name 'Ahalya' traditionally signify?
options: One who is faultless or unblemished
questions.explanation: The name Ahalya is often interpreted as 'one without blemish,' suggesting flawless beauty.

questions.q: On the road to Mithila, in whose hermitage region did Rama encounter the cursed Ahalya?
questions.explanation: Rama came upon Gautama's hermitage, which the sage had abandoned, where Ahalya lay as stone.

questions.q: In lineage tradition, the sage Gautama is counted in the line of which great rishi?
questions.explanation: Gautama is traditionally counted in the line of the sage Angiras.

questions.q: According to the Valmiki Ramayana, how did Gautama learn of Indra's deceit?
options: He encountered Indra fleeing in disguise
questions.explanation: Gautama, returning from his ablutions, encountered Indra and perceived the deceit.

questions.q: Which son of Gautama and Ahalya is well known in tradition as a court priest?
questions.explanation: Shatananda, the priest at King Janaka's court, is traditionally the son of Gautama and Ahalya.

questions.q: When Rama later reached Janaka's court at Mithila, who there was overjoyed to hear of Ahalya's liberation?
questions.explanation: Shatananda, the court priest and Ahalya's son, rejoiced upon hearing his mother had been freed.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, what condition did Gautama set for the end of Ahalya's curse?
options: When the Ganga reached the sea
When a thousand years had passed
When Rama would come to the hermitage
questions.explanation: Gautama declared the curse would end when Rama arrived and she received him.

questions.q: How did Rama and Lakshmana act toward Ahalya once she was restored?
options: They fought a demon guarding her
They reverently bowed and touched her feet
They recited the Vedas for a year
They built her a new shrine
questions.explanation: On her restoration, the princes paid their respects, touching the feet of the venerable Ahalya.

questions.q: What austerity did Ahalya reportedly maintain while cursed, according to the Valmiki tradition?
options: Standing on one foot in fire
Surviving on air, invisible and unseen
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes Ahalya enduring penance, surviving on air and remaining invisible during the curse.

questions.q: Which episode immediately precedes Rama's meeting with Ahalya in the Bala Kanda?
options: The slaying of Tataka and protection of the sacrifice
The building of the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: In the Bala Kanda, Rama protects Vishvamitra's sacrifice and slays the demoness Tataka before reaching Ahalya.
